task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the title 'NOC_System_Health_Wallboard' at the top of the Splunk dashboard page in the browser.

task_completion.description: The dashboard is built using Classic Splunk Dashboards, not Dashboard Studio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the standard Classic Splunk dashboard interface (white/gray background, standard panel borders, 'Edit' button top right). Ensure there is no Dashboard Studio grid canvas or Studio-specific configuration panel on the right side.

task_completion.description: A panel titled 'System Errors by Host' is created with the correct search query.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a panel titled 'System Errors by Host'. In the creation or edit steps, look for the exact search string: `index=system_logs ("error" OR "fail*" OR "fatal" OR "warn*") | stats count by host` in the search bar or panel configuration modal.

task_completion.description: The 'System Errors by Host' panel uses a color-coded Single Value visualization in a Trellis layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'System Errors by Host' panel. It must show a Trellis layout (a grid of multiple smaller charts within the single panel, one for each host). Each item in the grid must be a Single Value visualization (a large number) and must be color-coded (either the number itself or its background has a color like red, yellow, or green).

task_completion.description: A panel titled 'Log Volume Trend' is created with the correct search query.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a panel titled 'Log Volume Trend'. In the creation or edit steps, look for the exact search string: `index=system_logs | timechart count by host` in the search bar or panel configuration modal.

task_completion.description: The 'Log Volume Trend' panel uses a Stacked Column or Area Chart visualization.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Log Volume Trend' panel. The visualization must be either a Stacked Column chart (vertical bars stacked on top of each other) or an Area chart (filled line chart) showing data over time.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI in a web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the Splunk Enterprise web interface running in a browser at localhost:8000. The agent should not be simply writing XML files in a terminal text editor without verifying in the UI.

integrity.description: The dashboard panels render genuine Splunk visualizations with actual data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard Splunk UI elements in the charts, such as axes, legends, tooltips, or rendered numbers, indicating that Splunk actually executed the searches and rendered the results.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the Splunk interface by copy-pasting a pre-made dashboard XML directly from an external source without building or configuring it.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of the agent typing searches, selecting visualization types from the Splunk menus, or using the Splunk source editor legitimately.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is 'NOC_System_Health_Wallboard'. The underlying index to be queried is 'system_logs'. The Splunk credentials are 'admin' / 'SplunkAdmin1!'.
